name: Brno (Czechia)
description: A heraldic banner of the city's arms: a horizontal quadband of white, red, white and red, with the top white stripe half the width of the other three (stripe widths 1:2:2:2).
history: The flag is a plain banner of the historic municipal arms — a shield barry of four silver and red in the proportions 1:2:2:2, whose oldest depiction (on a town seal) dates to 1315. The flag was granted to the statutory city by decision of the Speaker of the Chamber of Deputies of the Parliament of the Czech Republic on 17 May 1994.

features.symbolism: a heraldic banner of the municipal arms — four silver-and-red bars, the city's colours since the Middle Ages
